Moreover Job continued his parable, and said,
Moreover the Lord answered Job, and said,

time will I wait, till my change come. Thou shalt call, and I will answer thee: thou wilt have a desire to the work of thine hands.” Chapter xiv, 12-15. Read again in the 19th chapter, where he is a little more explicit, commencing at the 23rd verse: “Oh that my words were now written! oh that they were printed in a book! That they were graven with an iron pen and lead in the rock forever! For I…
So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
Job speaks of his standing on the earth in the latter day, and says, ** in my flesh I shall see God.*’
Oh that I were as in months past, as in the days when God preserved me;
You were preserved, midst Babylonish night. From atheistic and sectarian blight; From manly rectitude you did not swerve. The Priest of Baal you never stooped to serve.
Now therefore be not grieved, nor angry with yourselves, that ye sold me hither: for God did send me before you to preserve life.

Now it came to pass in the thirtieth year, in the fourth month, in the fifth day of the month, as I was among the captives by the river of Chebar, that the heavens were opened, and I saw visions of God.

dwells within the tabernacle, is a being that has capacity sufficient to retain all its past doings, whether they be good or bad. It is, then, this memory that will produce the suffering and the pains upon that class of spirits whose works have been wicked and abominable in the sight of God. A spirit, then, will remember, that “at such a time in yonder world, and at such a place, I disobeyed the…
I remembered thy judgments of old, O Lord; and have comforted myself.
When his candle shined upon my head, and when by his light I walked through darkness;
He hath led me, and brought me into darkness, but not into light.
For thou wilt light my candle: the Lord my God will enlighten my darkness.
The light shall be dark in his tabernacle, and his candle shall be put out with him.
¶ No man, when he hath lighted a candle, covereth it with a vessel, or putteth it under a bed; but setteth it on a candlestick, that they which enter in may see the light.
As I was in the days of my youth, when the secret of God was upon my tabernacle;
Knowing that shortly I must put off this my tabernacle, even as our Lord Jesus Christ hath shewed me.
And I that am the Lord thy God from the land of Egypt will yet make thee to dwell in tabernacles, as in the days of the solemn feast.

Solomon in the days of old. When Solomon had built the temple, he spread forth his hands to the heavens, and prayed to the Father, in the presence of the congregation of Israel that was assembled, and the spirit of the Lord was poured out in such a wonderful manner that the people, through their faith, beheld the power and the glory of God as they were manifested in that temple. By this the…
For in the time of trouble he shall hide me in his pavilion: in the secret of his tabernacle shall he hide me; he shall set me up upon a rock.
Now when these things were thus ordained, the priests went always into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the service of God.
When the Almighty was yet with me, when my children were about me;
My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you,
That your generations may know that I made the children of Israel to dwell in booths, when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
And I bowed down my head, and worshipped the Lord, and blessed the Lord God of my master Abraham, which had led me in the right way to take my master’s brother’s daughter unto his son.

14 And I knew, my children, that because of Joseph this had befallen me, and I repented and wept; and I besought the Lord God that my hand might be restored and that I might hold aloof from all pollution and envy and from all folly.
When I washed my steps with butter, and the rock poured me out rivers of oil;
I have digged and drunk strange waters, and with the sole of my feet have I dried up all the rivers of besieged places.
And it came to pass that I did make tools of the ore which I did molten out of the rock.
I have digged, and drunk water; and with the sole of my feet have I dried up all the rivers of the besieged places.
He brought me up also out of an horrible pit, out of the miry clay, and set my feet upon a rock, and established my goings.

Morning fine with ice a quarter of an inch thick on the water pails.
Walked on the mountain east with President Young and Kimball, from
whence we had a pleasing view of the surrounding valley which is
about ten miles wide. Abundance of timber on the mountains south
and southwest and beyond that plenty of snow. After having prayers,
we again descended and at the foot discovered a very strong…
When I went out to the gate through the city, when I prepared my seat in the street!

I arrived home about the middle of the night, and furnishing my
wife with a horse, we made our escape in safety.
The young men saw me, and hid themselves: and the aged arose, and stood up.
There were among you adulterers and adulteresses; some of whom have turned away from you, and others remain with you that hereafter shall be revealed.
And the woman took the two men, and hid them, and said thus, There came men unto me, but I wist not whence they were:
But he forsook the counsel of the old men, which they had given him, and consulted with the young men that were grown up with him, and which stood before him:
And the young men arose, wound him up, and carried him out, and buried him.

{208} 14. There were among you adulterers and adulteresses; some
    of whom have turned away from you, and others remain with you that
    hereafter shall be revealed.
The princes refrained talking, and laid their hand on their mouth.

But if the princes hear that I have talked with thee, and they come unto thee, and say unto thee, Declare unto us now what thou hast said unto the king, hide it not from us, and we will not put thee to death; also what the king said unto thee:

Then came all the princes unto Jeremiah, and asked him: and he told them according to all these words that the king had commanded. So they left off speaking with him; for the matter was not perceived.
The nobles held their peace, and their tongue cleaved to the roof of their mouth.
And the princes said unto them, Let them live; but let them be hewers of wood and drawers of water unto all the congregation; as the princes had promised them.
And they brought forth the he goats for the sin offering before the king and the congregation; and they laid their hands upon them:
The nobles held their peace, and their tongue cleaved to the roof of their mouth.
And the high priest Ananias commanded them that stood by him to smite him on the mouth.
The princes refrained talking, and laid their hand on their mouth.
The princes digged the well, the nobles of the people digged it, by the direction of the lawgiver, with their staves. And from the wilderness they went to Mattanah:

And they did land upon the shore of the promised land. And when they had set their feet upon the shores of the promised land they bowed themselves down upon the face of the land, and did humble themselves before the Lord, and did shed tears of joy before the Lord, because of the multitude of his tender mercies over them.
When the ear heard me, then it blessed me; and when the eye saw me, it gave witness to me:
But blessed are your eyes, for they see: and your ears, for they hear.
Then the spirit took me up, and I heard behind me a voice of a great rushing, saying, Blessed be the glory of the Lord from his place.
Unto me men gave ear, and waited, and kept silence at my counsel.
I cried unto God with my voice, even unto God with my voice; and he gave ear unto me.
Because I delivered the poor that cried, and the fatherless, and him that had none to help him.
Did not I weep for him that was in trouble? was not my soul grieved for the poor?
So that they cause the cry of the poor to come unto him, and he heareth the cry of the afflicted.
Because he slew me not from the womb; or that my mother might have been my grave, and her womb to be always great with me.
They cried, but there was none to save them: even unto the Lord, but he answered them not.
The blessing of him that was ready to perish came upon me: and I caused the widow’s heart to sing for joy.

And it came to pass that after we had come down into the wilderness unto our father, behold, he was filled with joy, and also my mother, Sariah, was exceedingly glad, for she truly had mourned because of us.

receive any benefit from medicine, and for this cause he discontinued his attendance. The ensuing night, she lay altogether motionless, with her eyes wide open, and with that peculiar aspect which bespeaks the near approach of death. As she thus lay, I gazed upon her as a mother looks upon the last shade of life in a darling child. In this moment of distraction, my hnsband and myself clasped our…
I put on righteousness, and it clothed me: my judgment was as a robe and a diadem.
For he put on righteousness as a breastplate, and an helmet of salvation upon his head; and he put on the garments of vengeance for clothing, and was clad with zeal as a cloak.

Wherefore, we shall have a perfect knowledge of all our guilt, and our uncleanness, and our nakedness; and the righteous shall have a perfect knowledge of their enjoyment, and their righteousness, being clothed with purity, yea, even with the robe of righteousness.

prison and from judgment: and who shall declare his generation? for he was cut off out of the land of the living: for the transgressions of my people was he stricken,” &c. Again, the same Prophet spoke of him as coming in power, glory and dominion, and as having his wrath and indignation kindled against the nations of the earth. “Who is this that cometh from Edom, with dyed garments from Bozrah?…
¶ And moreover I saw under the sun the place of judgment, that wickedness was there; and the place of righteousness, that iniquity was there.
Thus saith the Lord, Keep ye judgment, and do justice: for my salvation is near to come, and my righteousness to be revealed.
I was eyes to the blind, and feet was I to the lame.
And the blind and the lame came to him in the temple; and he healed them.

For I Was Blind, but Now I See By President Thomas S. Monson First Counselor in the First Presidency If you want to give a light to others, you have to glow yourself. When Jesus walked and taught among men, He spoke in language easily understood. Whether He was journeying along the dusty way from Perea to Jerusalem, addressing the multitudes on the shore of the Sea of Galilee, or pausing beside…
But as for me, my feet were almost gone; my steps had well nigh slipped.
We grope for the wall like the blind, and we grope as if we had no eyes: we stumble at noonday as in the night; we are in desolate places as dead men.
And when he had spoken such words unto me, I set my face toward the ground, and I became dumb.
I was a father to the poor: and the cause which I knew not I searched out.
Defend the poor and fatherless: do justice to the afflicted and needy.
The righteous considereth the cause of the poor: but the wicked regardeth not to know it.
Because I delivered the poor that cried, and the fatherless, and him that had none to help him.

My father, Solomon Mack, was born in the town of Lyme, New London
county, State of Connecticut, September 26, 1735. His father, Ebenezer
Mack, was a man of considerable property, and lived in good style,
commanding all the attention and respect which are ever shown to those
who live in fine circumstances, and habits of strict morality. For a
length of time he fully enjoyed the fruits of his…
And I brake the jaws of the wicked, and plucked the spoil out of his teeth.
When the wicked, even mine enemies and my foes, came upon me to eat up my flesh, they stumbled and fell.
Such is the way of an adulterous woman; she eateth, and wipeth her mouth, and saith, I have done no wickedness.
And I went out after him, and smote him, and delivered it out of his mouth: and when he arose against me, I caught him by his beard, and smote him, and slew him.
He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones, he hath covered me with ashes.
And he said, This is wickedness. And he cast it into the midst of the ephah; and he cast the weight of lead upon the mouth thereof.
Then I said, I shall die in my nest, and I shall multiply my days as the sand.
And every one that heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them not, shall be likened unto a foolish man, which built his house upon the sand:
As the host of heaven cannot be numbered, neither the sand of the sea measured: so will I multiply the seed of David my servant, and the Levites that minister unto me.
And every one that heareth these sayings of mine and doeth them not shall be likened unto a foolish man, who built his house upon the sand—
And I, God, blessed them, saying: Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the sea; and let fowl multiply in the earth;
That your days may be multiplied, and the days of your children, in the land which the Lord sware unto your fathers to give them, as the days of heaven upon the earth.
My root was spread out by the waters, and the dew lay all night upon my branch.
And behold this last, whose branch hath withered away, I did plant in a good spot of ground; yea, even that which was choice unto me above all other parts of the land of my vineyard.
His roots shall be dried up beneath, and above shall his branch be cut off.

But if ye neglect the tree, and take no thought for its nourishment, behold it will not get any root; and when the heat of the sun cometh and scorcheth it, because it hath no root it withers away, and ye pluck it up and cast it out.
I made me pools of water, to water therewith the wood that bringeth forth trees:
My glory was fresh in me, and my bow was renewed in my hand.
Before whose throne all things bow in humble reverence, and give him glory forever and ever.
And the bow shall be in the cloud; and I will look upon it, that I may remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is upon the earth.
I do set my bow in the cloud, and it shall be for a token of a covenant between me and the earth.
Unto me men gave ear, and waited, and kept silence at my counsel.
I waited patiently for the Lord; and he inclined unto me, and heard my cry.
But the Lord was wroth with me for your sakes, and would not hear me: and the Lord said unto me, Let it suffice thee; speak no more unto me of this matter.
And when I did turn unto my closet, O Lord, and prayed unto thee, thou didst hear me.
In my distress I cried unto the Lord, and he heard me.
After my words they spake not again; and my speech dropped upon them.

And I did rehearse unto them the words of Isaiah, who spake concerning the restoration of the Jews, or of the house of Israel; and after they were restored they should no more be confounded, neither should they be scattered again. And it came to pass that I did speak many words unto my brethren, that they were pacified and did humble themselves before the Lord.

And the Lord heard the voice of your words, when ye spake unto me; and the Lord said unto me, I have heard the voice of the words of this people, which they have spoken unto thee: they have well said all that they have spoken.
And it came to pass that after I had received strength I spake unto my brethren, desiring to know of them the cause of their disputations.
And my speech and my preaching was not with enticing words of man’s wisdom, but in demonstration of the Spirit and of power:
I beheld the transgressors, and was grieved; because they kept not thy word.
And they waited for me as for the rain; and they opened their mouth wide as for the latter rain.
And caused me to pass by them round about: and, behold, there were very many in the open valley; and, lo, they were very dry.

And also I have withholden the rain from you, when there were yet three months to the harvest: and I caused it to rain upon one city, and caused it not to rain upon another city: one piece was rained upon, and the piece whereupon it rained not withered.
They gaped upon me with their mouths, as a ravening and a roaring lion.
And I saw when the Lamb opened one of the seals, and I heard, as it were the noise of thunder, one of the four beasts saying, Come and see.
They came upon me as a wide breaking in of waters: in the desolation they rolled themselves upon me.
If I laughed on them, they believed it not; and the light of my countenance they cast not down.
And they that were with me saw indeed the light, and were afraid; but they heard not the voice of him that spake to me.

And because they did cast them all out, that there were none righteous among them, I did send down fire and destroy them, that their wickedness and abominations might be hid from before my face, that the blood of the prophets and the saints whom I sent among them might not cry unto me from the ground against them.

And they understood me not that I said they shall hear my voice; and they understood me not that the Gentiles should not at any time hear my voice—that I should not manifest myself unto them save it were by the Holy Ghost.
And it came to pass that I called after them, and they did hear me; wherefore they did cease to flee from my presence.
And also because I said unto them that they were a lost and a fallen people they were angry with me, and sought to lay their hands upon me, that they might cast me into prison.
I chose out their way, and sat chief, and dwelt as a king in the army, as one that comforteth the mourners.
So Sennacherib king of Assyria departed, and went and returned, and dwelt at Nineveh.
¶ Then I came to the governors beyond the river, and gave them the king’s letters. Now the king had sent captains of the army and horsemen with me.
